title: Herbal Teas for Asthma: The Top 10 Choices
intro_desc: Herbal teas for asthma are natural remedies that help alleviate asthma symptoms by opening airways, reducing inflammation, and improving respiratory function. The best herbal teas for asthma include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Rosmarinus officinalis, Thymus vulgaris, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Lavandula angustifolia, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Pelargonium sidoides, and Verbascum thapsus. If left untreated, asthma can severely impact daily life, leading to frequent hospitalizations, missed work or school, and disrupted social activities, ultimately affecting mental and physical well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is good for people with asthma because it has special properties that help to open up the airways in the lungs. When you drink this tea, the active ingredients, especially eucalyptol, spread through your body and reach your lungs. Eucalyptol is like a key that unlocks the airways, making it easier to breathe. It also helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, which is common in people with asthma. This means that you can breathe more easily and feel better when you're drinking Eucalyptus globulus tea. Additionally, the tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. By drinking this tea regularly, you can help to control your asthma and reduce your symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for asthma because it helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of wheezing and coughing.
Cineole is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Catechins are good for asthma because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Eucalyptol is good for asthma because it helps to relax the airways and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for asthma
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Pelargonium sidoides herbal tea that may help with asthma, start by gathering dried root of the Pelargonium sidoides plant, also known as Umckaloabo. You can purchase this herb from a health food store or online. Begin by boiling one liter of water, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add one to two teaspoons of dried Pelargonium sidoides root to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. You can also add other herbs like honey and lemon to taste. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. To use the tea for asthma, drink one to two cups a day, ideally before meals. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, especially if you have severe asthma or are taking other medications. They can help you determine the correct dosage and ensure the tea won't interact with your medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea, also known as thyme tea, is a natural remedy that may help with asthma symptoms. Thyme contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the airways and make it easier to breathe. The tea also contains antioxidants that can help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. When inhaled, the steam from thyme tea may help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thyme has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off infections that can trigger asthma attacks. Drinking thyme tea may also help relax the airway muscles, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing and coughing.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and open up airways, making it easier to breathe.
Carvacrol is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Rosmarinic acid is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Quercetin is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and ease breathing difficulties.
Tannins are good for asthma because they help reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieve symptoms of asthma.

remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for asthma
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Pelargonium sidoides herbal teas that help with asthma, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Pelargonium sidoides leaves, which can be purchased from a health food store or online. Next, boil some water in a kettle or on the stove. While the water is heating up, add one teaspoon of dried Pelargonium sidoides leaves to a cup or teapot. Once the water has reached a boiling point, carefully pour it over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the tea will be. After the steeping time has passed, carefully strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. Finally, drink the tea while it is still warm and enjoy the potential benefits it may have on your asthma symptoms. It's worth noting that you should consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have a severe case of asthma or are taking any medication.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea has been used for centuries to help with breathing problems like asthma. The tea is made from the flowers and roots of the Echinacea plant, which contain special compounds that can help calm and open up airways. When you drink Echinacea purpurea tea, these compounds work to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful during an asthma attack, when airways can become narrow and swollen, making breathing difficult. Echinacea purpurea tea may also have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the lungs, reducing the need for medication and other treatments. By reducing inflammation and opening up airways, Echinacea purpurea tea can provide quick relief from asthma symptoms, helping you feel better and breathe easier.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for asthma because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the airways and make breathing easier.
Quercetin is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and make breathing easier.
Cichoric acid is good for asthma because it helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for asthma
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Pelargonium sidoides herbal tea that may help with asthma, start by gathering dried roots and leaves of the Pelargonium sidoides plant. You can purchase this herbal ingredient from a health store or online. Next, measure one to two teaspoons of the dried plant material and place it in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Then, boil some water and let it cool for a minute to prevent the tea from becoming too bitter. Pour the boiling water over the herb and let it steep for five to seven minutes. Strain the tea into another cup using the tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the herb. Finally, drink the warm tea two to three times a day, ideally after meals. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have a severe asthma condition or are taking medication.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary tea, is a herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to help manage asthma symptoms. The main reason rosemary tea is good for asthma is that it contains compounds called antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation in the lungs. When you have asthma, your airways become inflamed and swollen, making it hard to breathe. Rosemary tea's antioxidants work to calm down this inflammation, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, rosemary tea contains a compound called camphor, which helps to open up the airways and make it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful during asthma attacks, when breathing can be very difficult. By drinking rosemary tea, people with asthma may find that their symptoms improve and they are able to breathe more easily.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid helps to fight asthma because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the airways and make it easier to breathe.
Carnosic acid is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and open up airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Quercetin is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and calm the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Luteolin is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and make breathing easier.

remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for asthma
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Pelargonium sidoides herbal tea that helps with asthma, you will need dried Pelargonium sidoides roots and leaves. First, boil fresh water in a pot. Then, add one teaspoon of dried Pelargonium sidoides roots and leaves to the boiling water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 5-7 minutes. You can also add a little honey to sweeten the tea if you prefer. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the roots and leaves. Let the tea cool down for a few minutes before drinking it. It's recommended to drink the tea 2-3 times a day for best results. However, please consult with a healthcare professional before using Pelargonium sidoides herbal tea for asthma treatment, especially if you are already taking any medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is often used to help people with asthma. This is because the leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain special compounds that can help open up airways in the lungs. When you breathe, air flows through these airways and into your lungs. But for people with asthma, the airways can become narrow or swollen, making it harder to breathe. The compounds in Ginkgo biloba tea can help relax and widen these airways, making it easier for air to flow in and out of the lungs. This can help reduce symptoms of asthma, such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the airways and make it easier to breathe.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in airways and prevent bronchospasms, making it easier to breathe.
Ginkgolide B is good for asthma because it helps to block the chemical called leukotriene, which can cause inflammation and narrowing of airways, making it easier to breathe.
Kaempferol is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and ease breathing.

remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for asthma
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Pelargonium sidoides herbal tea that may help with asthma, start by gathering dried Pelargonium sidoides roots, which are also known as Umckaloabo. Use one teaspoon of the dried roots for every cup of boiling water. Next, place the dried roots in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup and pour in boiling water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes, which will help the roots release their active ingredients into the water. After the steeping time has passed, strain the liquid using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the roots. You can then discard the roots and drink the tea, which can be taken 2-3 times a day. It's essential to note that while Pelargonium sidoides herbal tea may help alleviate asthma symptoms, it should not be used as a replacement for conventional medical treatment. Always consult with a healthcare professional before trying any new herbal remedy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a traditional remedy for asthma. It contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink this tea, the glycyrrhizin helps to reduce inflammation in your airways, making it easier to breathe. This is especially helpful during asthma attacks, when airways become narrow and swollen, causing difficulty in breathing. Glycyrrhizin also has a natural expectorant effect, which means it helps to loosen and clear mucus from your lungs, making it easier to cough up. Additionally, the tea has been shown to help relax the muscles in your airways, making it even easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful for people with asthma who experience shortness of breath or wheezing. By reducing inflammation, loosening mucus, and relaxing airway muscles, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea may help to alleviate asthma symptoms.

Glycyrrhizin is good for asthma because it helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Polyphenols are good for asthma because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, making breathing easier for people with asthma.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Pelargonium sidoides herbal tea that helps with asthma, first gather the required ingredients, including dried roots or leaves of the Pelargonium sidoides plant. You can purchase these at a health food store or online. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a pot, then remove from heat. Add 1-2 teaspoons of the dried plant material to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. This allows the active compounds to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, strain the liquid into a cup, making sure to discard the solids. You can then add honey or lemon to taste, although it's best to avoid adding anything that might irritate your asthma. Once the tea has cooled down, drink it slowly and steadily, ideally 2-3 times a day for the best benefits. It's essential to note that while Pelargonium sidoides tea may help alleviate asthma symptoms, it's not a replacement for medical treatment. Consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, especially if you're taking any medications or have severe asthma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as lavender tea, is a natural remedy that may help people with asthma. The tea is made from the flowers of the lavender plant, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. When we have asthma, our airways get swollen and inflamed, which makes it hard to breathe. The anti-inflammatory properties in lavender tea may help reduce this swelling and make it easier to breathe. Additionally, lavender tea has a calming effect, which can also help reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger asthma attacks. By drinking lavender tea regularly, people with asthma may find that it helps them breathe easier and have fewer symptoms. The tea is also easy to make at home by steeping dried lavender flowers in hot water. This makes it a convenient and natural way to help manage asthma symptoms.

Linalyl acetate is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax the airways and reduce swelling, making it easier to breathe.
Rosmarinic acid is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Pelargonium sidoides herbal tea that may help with asthma, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried roots and leaves of the Pelargonium sidoides plant, which can be found at most health food stores or online. First, boil one cup of water and then remove it from heat. Next, add one to two teaspoons of the dried plant material to the hot water, and let it steep for five to ten minutes. This allows the active ingredients to be released into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the plant material, and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming the tea, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies. It's also essential to note that Pelargonium sidoides tea may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a type of herb that has been used for centuries to help with various health problems. The tea made from this herb is said to be good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The tea also has a calming effect, which can help to relax the muscles in the airways and improve breathing. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of Melissa officinalis tea can help to stop muscle spasms in the airways, which can trigger asthma attacks. Drinking this tea may help to reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks, making it a potential natural remedy for people who suffer from this condition.

You can buy these online or at a local health store. Begin by boiling one cup of water in a pot. Once the water is boiling, add one to two teaspoons of dried Pelargonium sidoides leaves and flowers to the water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the water. remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a type of herb that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues, including asthma. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways. This makes it easier to breathe and can help alleviate asthma symptoms like wheezing and coughing. Ginger also helps relax the muscles in the airways, making them less likely to constrict and trigger asthma attacks. Additionally, ginger has natural expectorant properties, which means it can help bring up mucus and phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and breathe. Drinking Zingiber officinale tea may also help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to asthma symptoms.

Gingerols are good for asthma because they help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms of the condition.
Polyphenols are good for asthma because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for asthma
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Pelargonium sidoides herbal tea that helps with asthma, you will need dried Pelargonium sidoides roots. First, boil a cup of water in a kettle or on the stove, and then add one to two teaspoons of dried Pelargonium sidoides roots to the boiling water. Let it simmer for about 5 to 10 minutes, allowing the roots to release their medicinal properties into the water. Next, turn off the heat and let the tea steep for another 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to combine and the tea to become strong. Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth into a cup, and discard the roots. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but make sure to drink it while it's still warm to get the most benefits. It is recommended to drink two to three cups of this tea per day to help alleviate asthma symptoms. However, consult with a healthcare professional or a registered herbalist before using this tea as a treatment, as it may interact with other medications or have side effects in certain individuals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pelargonium sidoides tea is a herbal remedy that may help people with asthma breathe easier. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the airways. When airways are swollen, it's harder to breathe. The tea also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to cough up. Thicker mucus can block airways and make breathing difficult. Pelargonium sidoides contains a compound called geraniol, which has been shown to help relax airway muscles. When these muscles relax, airways open up, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the tea may help to reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects. Some studies have suggested that the tea may be as effective as conventional asthma medications in reducing symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and make breathing easier.
Rosmarinic acid is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and make it easier to breathe.
Kaempferol is good for asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and airway constriction, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Pelargonium sidoides herbal teas for asthma
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Pelargonium sidoides herbal tea that helps with asthma, start by gathering the required ingredients, which include dried Pelargonium sidoides roots or leaves. Begin by boiling a cup of water, then add one teaspoon of the dried roots or leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-10 minutes. After the steeping time, strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a tea strainer to remove the plant material. Next, add honey or sugar to taste, if desired, as the bitterness of the tea can be quite strong. Once the tea has cooled down a bit, it's ready to drink. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of this tea per day to help alleviate asthma symptoms, but consult a doctor or a qualified healthcare professional before making any changes to your treatment plan. Also, note that the effectiveness of this tea may vary from person to person, and it's not a replacement for medical treatment.

supplementary_best: For asthma relief, a combination of herbal teas is more effective than one herb alone. The best combination is Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, and Pelargonium sidoides. Eucalyptus globulus helps loosen and clear mucus from airways, making it easier to breathe. Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in airways and prevent asthma attacks. Pelargonium sidoides, also known as umckaloabo, has been used traditionally to treat respiratory infections and asthma, as it helps to thin mucus and reduce inflammation. This combination is the best because it addresses multiple aspects of asthma: it helps to clear mucus, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. Drinking these teas together will provide comprehensive relief from asthma symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have asthma, there are some herbal teas you should avoid.  Yarrow (Achillea millefolium) tea can cause allergic reactions in some people, which can trigger asthma symptoms.  Sage (Salvia officinalis) tea may not be suitable for people with asthma. Sage contains a compound that can cause bronchoconstriction, which means the airways narrow and make breathing harder. This can trigger an asthma attack.  Marshmallow root (Althaea officinalis) tea may not be the best choice for people with asthma. The root can cause inflammation in the body, which can worsen asthma symptoms.  Butterbur (Petasites hybridus) tea can cause problems for people with asthma. The herb contains a compound that can cause blood vessels to constrict, which can worsen asthma symptoms.
supplementary_other_remedies: Certain natural remedies can help with asthma by reducing inflammation and opening up airways. For example, inhaling steam from a bowl of hot water can help loosen mucus and make it easier to breathe. Adding eucalyptus oil to the water can make it even more effective, as eucalyptus has natural decongestant properties. Another option is to use a humidifier in the home, which can add moisture to the air and help prevent dry airways. Some people also find relief from asthma symptoms by using a nebulizer, a device that turns medicine into a mist that can be breathed in. This can help reduce inflammation and open up airways. Additionally, some studies suggest that acupuncture and acupressure may also help reduce asthma symptoms by stimulating certain points on the body that can help relax the airways.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with asthma often experience other health issues due to inflammation and immune system problems. These issues may include allergies, sinusitis, and bronchitis. Medicinal herbs can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions. For example, Butterbur (Petasites hybridus) has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, relieving symptoms of sinusitis. Turmeric contains curcumin, a compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and ease symptoms of bronchitis. Echinacea is known to boost the immune system, reducing the risk of infections that can trigger asthma attacks. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis. These herbs can be used in various forms, such as teas, capsules, or tinctures, and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
